checks.finding: No issues found. T-001 (Risk: medium) edits scripts/bump-version.sh with a strictly additive, fail-closed precondition (design.md API/Contract Plan) and T-002 (Risk: medium) adds .github/workflows/release.yml's loop-gate job with no elevated permissions (security-spec.md B4). Neither task's Scope/Done When/Goal touches authentication, authorization, payment, PII storage, data migration, or an externally-visible API contract per risk-classification-policy.md's sensitive-surface list; both Risk Rationale sections (tasks.md T-001/T-002) argue medium correctly (additive-only, no existing mutation/validation logic modified, no elevated permission grant). No over-classification concern since neither task is high/critical.
SKIP: no Risk: high or Risk: critical task exists in tasks.md -- T-001 and T-002 are both Risk: medium, so this check's scoped surface is absent.

T-001's Done When section (tasks.md, T-001) lists 11 distinct '- [ ]' checkbox items (TEST-001..TEST-006, Commit B bullets 1-4, and the acceptance-first evidence item), exceeding the '>8 distinct verifiable items' oversizing indicator. Two bullets each bundle two claims via 'AND' (Commit B bullet 3: 'scripts/ contains no bump-version.ps1, AND the runbook states...'; Commit B bullet 4: 'README.md is verified..., and tests/validate-repository.sh exits 0...'), pushing the true atomic-claim count to ~13 and undercutting the round-2 remedy's own stated goal ('1 bullet = 1 verifiable claim'). The round-1 finding (Reviewer B, TASK-SIZE Major) targeted a different oversizing symptom (Scope spanning 4 implementation areas in a single commit); the round-2 two-commit split reduced single-diff review risk but did not reduce T-001's total verification surface -- it remains one task ID, one Approval gate, one Risk tier, evaluated as a whole by quality-gate. T-001's ### Scope section (containing both Commit A and Commit B under one heading) also still spans 4 distinct implementation areas (bash script edit, new .sh/.ps1 test-suite authoring, CI registration in run-all.sh/.ps1 + test.yml, and documentation across CHANGELOG.md/release-runbook.md/README.md). T-002's Done When list has 7 items (under the 8-item threshold) and is not flagged.

No issues found. T-001's functional scope has associated error-path acceptance criteria AC-002/AC-003 (loop-consistency and loop-inventory each independently stubbed to fail, asserting exit 1 and zero mutation) and AC-004 (no-bypass negative grep check), acceptance-tests.md. T-002's functional scope has AC-008 (escape-hatch/`continue-on-error`/`if: always()` absence scan) and AC-009 (negative-branch canary on a `needs:`-stripped fixture). Both functional tasks have edge-case/error-path coverage beyond the happy path (AC-001, AC-007).

No issues found. Each TEST-NNN's declared type matches its described scope: TEST-001 ('integration (fixture-driven, real script)') drives the real bump-version.sh against real/stub loop suites inside a git-init'ed fixture -- at least two system components; TEST-002/TEST-003 are the red-path variants of the same multi-component fixture; TEST-004/TEST-005 are single-file (scripts/bump-version.sh) static checks, appropriately unit-scoped; TEST-007/TEST-008/TEST-010 are single-file (release.yml) structural checks; TEST-009 tests the marker-check function's discriminating power on a mutated fixture; TEST-011/TEST-012/TEST-014 ('document conformance') each map to a specific AC-NNN (AC-011/AC-012/AC-014, acceptance-tests.md table) as the acceptance-type check requires. No declared type contradicts its described scope.

SKIP: no Risk: high or Risk: critical task exists (T-001, T-002 both Risk: medium), so this check's mandated surface is absent. Both tasks voluntarily carry a Rollback: field (two-commit joint revert), but this is not required by the check for medium-risk tasks.

No issues found. T-001 and T-002 share five Planned Files (tests/run-all.sh, tests/run-all.ps1, .github/workflows/test.yml, CHANGELOG.md, docs/contributor/release-runbook.md), but every overlap is coordinated: T-002's Blockers field names T-001, and Global Constraints (tasks.md/design.md) specify line-additive, disjoint registration edits plus an explicit CREATE (T-001) / APPEND (T-002) convention for CHANGELOG.md and release-runbook.md, matching the check's Blockers-enforced exemption. No uncoordinated parallel overlap exists.

No issues found. T-002's Blockers: T-001 is not spurious: although T-002's own 'Depends On' note concedes its release.yml text-marker test content has no functional dependency on T-001's script edit, the blocker is justified by genuine shared-artifact collision risk -- the CHANGELOG.md/release-runbook.md create-then-append ordering (T-002 cannot append to an entry T-001 has not yet created) and the run-all.sh/.ps1 + test.yml array-registration serialization Global Constraints calls out to avoid a merge collision. No missing-blocker gap: T-001 correctly declares 'Depends On: none' / 'Blockers: None'.
SKIP: no bugfix, regression-fix, debugging, flaky-test-resolution, or incident-remediation task exists in tasks.md -- T-001 and T-002 are both net-new feature/wiring additions.
